1.question: Legacy Express v4 route: app.get('/data', async (req,res,next) => { const d = await fetchFromDB(); res.json(d); }). If fetchFromDB rejects, what happens?
options: Express returns a 404.
Client gets a generic 500 automatically.
None of the above.
Express auto-catches and forwards to the (err,req,res,next) error middleware.
The request hangs until timeout; an UnhandledPromiseRejection warning is logged and may crash the Node process.

2.question: GET /transactions over 50M rows with ?limit=100&offset=4999900 takes 8s. Standard API design fix?
options: Cursor-based pagination (?limit=100&after=cursor_id) — DB uses an index to jump to the start instead of scanning + skipping millions.
Cache all 50M rows in Redis.
Use SELECT COUNT(*) to pre-calculate all pages.
Raise limit to 10,000 so fewer requests are needed.
None of the above.

3.question: Stripe webhooks: attacker replays a valid payload 500x to double-credit. Fundamental fix?
options: Verify the cryptographic Stripe-Signature (which contains a timestamp) and reject payloads older than a few minutes.
None of the above.
Only allow POST, reject PUT.
Firewall to only accept webhooks from US IPs.
Validate JSON payload shape against a TS interface.

4.question: Node.js OOM crash; heap shows millions of trapped large arrays. Most likely culprit?
options: Recursive function without a base case (Call Stack Exceeded).
Forgetting new when instantiating an ES6 class.
An event listener on a long-lived object (e.g. process, server) closes over outer-scope vars, preventing GC.
None of the above.
JSON.parse on a very large response.

5.question: Prevent Lost Updates on PUT /articles/123 via Optimistic Concurrency. Which HTTP headers?
options: None of the above.
Strict-Transport-Security to encrypt the payload.
Server sends ETag; client sends Cache-Control: no-cache next time.
Server sends Authorization; client responds with X-Forwarded-For.
Server sends ETag (hash). Client sends If-Match with that hash on PUT. Server returns 412 Precondition Failed on mismatch.

6.question: Third-party API frequently returns 429 / 503. Industry-standard resilient integration pattern?
options: Exponential Backoff with Jitter — progressively increase wait + add randomness to avoid synchronized Thundering Herd.
setTimeout 60s then retry once.
None of the above.
Immediately return 200 to your client and drop the call.
while(true) retry every 10ms.

7.question: React app at http://localhost:3000 POSTs with a custom X-Tenant-ID header to http://api.example.com. Browser blocks: CORS preflight failed. What is actually failing?
options: Express is missing body-parser so the preflight JSON can't be read.
Axios is missing credentials: 'include'.
The OPTIONS preflight response is missing Access-Control-Allow-Headers: X-Tenant-ID, so the browser blocks the actual POST.
None of the above.
Server rejected because the frontend is not on HTTPS.

8.question: Express proxies a 5GB S3 video via const file = await s3.getObject(); res.send(file.body); — Node crashes. Correct rewrite?
options: Pipe the S3 readable stream directly into the Express response (s3Stream.pipe(res)) so chunks forward without buffering the whole file in RAM.
Download the file to local disk first then fs.readFileSync.
None of the above.
zlib-compress to ZIP before sending.
Bump heap to 8GB with --max-old-space-size=8192.

9.question: An endpoint does heavy synchronous math. All other clients experience massive latency and health-checks fail. Why?
options: GC pauses to clean math variables.
DB connection pool is exhausted.
Synchronous JS blocks the single-threaded Node Event Loop, preventing acceptance/processing of any other HTTP requests or async callbacks.
Router uses HTTP/1.1 instead of HTTP/2.
None of the above.

10.question: PATCH /users/:id does User.findByIdAndUpdate(req.params.id, req.body). Attacker sends {isAdmin:true} and becomes admin. Vulnerability + fix?
options: Mass Assignment / Over-posting — explicitly destructure permitted fields (e.g. const {name,email}=req.body) before update.
Broken Authentication — rotate JWT secret.
SQL Injection — escape input.
None of the above.
XSS — sanitize HTML in payload.

11.question: GraphQL fetches 50 users, then 50 separate Company queries — the N+1 problem. Standard fix?
options: Force the client to make separate REST calls.
None of the above.
Switch PostgreSQL to MongoDB.
Use Facebook's DataLoader to batch + cache the 50 calls into a single SELECT ... WHERE id IN (...).
Add a composite index on the Company table.

12.question: WebSocket chat drops after exactly 60s idle with no errors. Most likely cause?
options: Browser GC deleted the WebSocket variable.
None of the above.
An intermediate reverse proxy (Nginx / AWS LB) closed the idle TCP connection because no Ping/Pong heartbeats were sent.
React component unmounted in the background.
ws library has a hardcoded 60s cap.

13.question: POST /refunds — client may retry on timeout, risking double refund. Safe API design?
options: Require a unique Idempotency-Key header; if a successful txn with that key exists, return the cached response without re-processing.
Disable the user's account on duplicate payload.
None of the above.
Change to GET (naturally idempotent).
Rely on the DB primary key auto-increment.

14.question: Node worker uses a PG pool. After exactly 10 jobs it freezes (no CPU). What coding error?
options: Forgot pool.release() / client.end() inside a finally block — connection pool exhausted.
None of the above.
Queue payloads exceed 16MB.
PostgreSQL ran out of disk space.
Worker built with an older V8.

15.question: POST /users/batch with 1000 users; 999 valid, 1 invalid email. Most appropriate RESTful behavior?
options: Insert the 999 and return 207 Multi-Status with per-item 201 Created / 400 Bad Request details.
Halt, return 500, trigger PagerDuty.
None of the above.
Rollback all and return 400 so the client knows what failed.
Insert the 999, silently ignore the bad one, return 200.
